perturbation.original_text: At no time and under no circumstances shall the receiving Party reverse -engineer , decompile or disassemble any part of the Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: At no time and under no circumstances, unless required for regulatory compliance, shall the receiving Party reverse -engineer , decompile or disassemble any part of the Confidential Information .
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by allowing reverse engineering, decompiling, or disassembling of confidential information if it's required for regulatory compliance. This directly contradicts the original statement that prohibits these actions under 'no circumstances'. This can cause confusion about the extent of the restrictions and creates uncertainty about the enforceability of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information. The disclosing Party shall retain all right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information, including all intellectual property represented thereby or associated therew ith, and no license or assignment, by implication, estoppel or otherwise, is granted by the disclosing Party to the receiving Party to make, have made, use, or sell any product using the Confidential Information , or a license under any patent , patent applicat ion, utility model, copyright, trade secret, trademark, service mark or any other similar industrial or intellectual property right. At no time and under no circumstances, unless required for regulatory compliance, shall the receiving Party reverse -engineer , decompile or disassemble any part of the Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated an absolute prohibition on reverse engineering, decompiling, or disassembling confidential information. The changed text introduces an exception ('unless required for regulatory compliance'), directly contradicting the initial 'no circumstances' clause. The contradicted text includes the changed text, so it is restating a contradiction that was introduced. The contradiction creates uncertainty about the extent of confidentiality restrictions and therefore constitutes a strong in-text contradiction according to the provided definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any third party to whom such Party has provided Confidential Information, or any portion thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any third party to whom such Party has provided Confidential Information, or any portion thereof, unless the breach was unintentional and the third party is an immediate family member.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception to the responsibility clause, stating that a party is not responsible for breaches by immediate family members if the breach was unintentional. This contradicts the original clause, which holds each party responsible for any breach by third parties. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about the extent of liability and who bears the risk of unintentional disclosures by family members.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Responsibility for Breach. Each Party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any third party to whom such Party has provided Confidential Information, or any portion thereof. If a Party or any third party to whom such Party has provided Confidential Information becomes legally compelled (by oral question, deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process or by rule, regulation or other applicable law) to disclose any Confidential Information, such Party shall promptly notify the other Party of such requirement before any disclosure is made so that the other Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or may waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if a Party waives compliance with the provisions hereof, each Party agrees that only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is legally required to disclose (as advised by a written opinion of counsel) will be disclosed, and each Party agrees to exercise its best efforts to obtain assurance that the Confidential Information will be treated confidentially upon disclosur e.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the responsibility clause, stating that a party is not responsible for breaches by immediate family members if the breach was unintentional. This contradicts the original clause in both the original text and the contradicted text, which holds each party responsible for *any* breach by third parties. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding liability, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will continue in full force and effect until both parties agree in writing to terminate this agreement and for one -year thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will continue in full force and effect until both parties agree in writing to terminate this agreement and for one -year thereafter, except for customer lists, which shall remain confidential for two years thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a contradiction regarding the agreement's duration. While the general agreement terminates one year after written termination, customer lists are stipulated to remain confidential for two years thereafter. This creates ambiguity concerning which duration applies to specific types of information, creating potential for disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. This Agreement will continue in full force and effect until both parties agree in writing to terminate this agreement and for one -year thereafter. The Parties agree that if the negotiated transaction does not proceed within a reasonable time, or upon any request from either Party, each Party shall promptly deliver to the other Party all written Confidential Information and any other written material containing or reflecting Confidential Information and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. All documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by each Party, or respective representatives, based on the information included in the Confidential Information shall be pro mptly destroyed, and such destruction shall be ce1tified in writing by an authorized officer supervising such destruction. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of any Confidential Information, each Party shall continue to be bound by its obligations o f confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement terminates one year after written termination. The changed text introduces an exception: customer lists remain confidential for two years. This contradicts the initial statement in both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text because both state that the agreement ends after 'one-year thereafter.' The Changed Text introduces a clause that extends a specific condition of the agreement beyond this one-year period, creating a strong inconsistency, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agreement by either Party, including, without limitation, the actual or threatened disclosure of a Party's Confidential Information without the express prior written consent of that Paity, such Party will suffer irreparable harm and injury and no remedy at law will afford it adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, such injury . Accordingly , each Party hereby agrees that, in any such event, the harmed Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance of the other Party's obligations under this Agreement , as well as such further injunctive relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agreement by either Party, including, without limitation, the actual or threatened disclosure of a Party's Confidential Information without the express prior written consent of that Paity, such Party will suffer irreparable harm and injury and no remedy at law will afford it adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, such injury, except for breaches related to inadvertent disclosure to immediate family members. Accordingly , each Party hereby agrees that, in any such event, the harmed Party shall be entitled to seek monetary damages only, not specific performance or injunctive relief, for breaches.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the form of relief available. The original text states that in the event of a breach (including unauthorized disclosure), the harmed party can seek specific performance and injunctive relief. However, the addition states that only monetary damages are available, specifically excluding specific performance and injunctive relief for all breaches. This creates direct conflict in what remedies are available, rendering the contract ambiguous and difficult to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Form of Relief. The Parties hereby acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agreement by either Party, including, without limitation, the actual or threatened disclosure of a Party's Confidential Information without the express prior written consent of that Paity, such Party will suffer irreparable harm and injury and no remedy at law will afford it adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, such injury, except for breaches related to inadvertent disclosure to immediate family members. Accordingly , each Party hereby agrees that, in any such event, the harmed Party shall be entitled to seek monetary damages only, not specific performance or injunctive relief, for breaches.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that in the event of a breach, including unauthorized disclosure, the harmed party is entitled to seek monetary damages only, not specific performance or injunctive relief. The contradicted text is identical, which means that the original text, allowing specific performance and injunctive relief for all breaches, is now directly contradicted by the changed text. The explanation validates this as the changed section completely changes the remedy available.
